evals.prompt: I just cloned this repo and want to understand it before diving into the code. What can you tell me about it?
evals.expected_output: A Repo Health Report with contribution style, risk matrix, bus factor, momentum, and firefighting sections — produced entirely from git history before reading any source files.
expectations: Runs git log --oneline before reading any source files (other than CONTRIBUTING.md or README for Phase 0)
Checks for CONTRIBUTING.md or README for declared commit conventions
Runs the churn hotspot command (git log --format=format: --name-only)
Runs git shortlog for bus factor with both all-time and 6-month windows
Uses learned commit keywords in grep, not hardcoded fix|bug|broken
Produces a Repo Health Report with a Contribution Style section
Does NOT read source files before completing the report

evals.prompt: Which files in this repo are the most risky or problematic? I want to know where to focus my attention.
evals.expected_output: A Risk Matrix table ranking files by compound score from both churn (git history) and bug commit density, with the highest-risk files called out clearly.
expectations: Runs the churn hotspot command (git log --format=format: --name-only)
Runs bug clustering command using learned keywords (not hardcoded fix|bug|broken)
Cross-references both lists to identify files appearing in BOTH top-churn and top-bug lists
Produces a Risk Matrix table with churn count and bug commit count columns
Ranks files by compound score, not just churn alone

evals.prompt: How does this team write commit messages? Is there a standard they follow?
evals.expected_output: A Contribution Style section that compares documented intent (CONTRIBUTING.md/README) vs. actual practice (sampled from git log), with real examples and a note on any drift or divergence.
expectations: Checks CONTRIBUTING.md for declared commit conventions
Also checks README for contribution guidelines
Samples git log --oneline -100 to observe actual commit practice
Compares declared vs. actual and notes if they diverge
Includes real examples extracted from the commit log
Notes if no convention is documented or consistently followed
Detects and reports drift if convention changed over time
